summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-002, a use variance request to allow an accessory dwelling unit (guest lodging) in the basement of an existing light manufacturing building on Charles Schneider Road. The applicant, Jerry Henline of HACO (formerly Henline Adhesive Equipment Company), sought to convert approximately 2,300 square feet of basement space into guest accommodations for customers and employees visiting the facility. After discussion of stipulations regarding temporary use and prohibition of for-profit rental, the board approved the use variance unanimously.
decisions: Approved minutes from the February 26th meeting
Approved use variance BZA 2024-002 with stipulations that the property not be used for for-profit rental and remain as a temporary use
action_items: Max Merritt/zoning staff to prepare the approved variance documentation and deliver to the applicant by end of the week
Applicant to go through Clermont County for building permits after receiving variance documentation
Township to consider a text amendment to address guest lodging facilities within the zoning code
key_amounts: Approximately 2,300 square feet proposed for the accessory dwelling unit
Approximately 3,600 square feet total in the lower basement area
800 square feet or 25% of floor area is the typical ADU maximum (this proposal exceeds that)
10,000 square feet minimum lot area requirement (met by the property)
42 years the business has been in the township
notable_topics: Use variance for accessory dwelling unit/guest lodging in M1 manufacturing zone
HACO's plan to convert basement space into guest accommodations for customers and employees
Discussion of stipulations to prevent permanent residence or for-profit rental use
Zoning code limitations on mixed uses in manufacturing districts vs. business districts
Potential future text amendment to address guest lodging in the zoning code
Vacancy concerns with BZA and Zoning Commission membership
attendees: Mr. Pruitt (BZA member - absent at roll call)
James Henline (potential heir to the business, 1321 Covedale Lane, Amelia, Ohio)
Unidentified resident from Phoenix Drive and Schneider Road area
notes: Mr. Pruitt was called during roll call but did not respond, suggesting absence. The board noted they are down one member on BZA and two on zoning commission. Staff did not provide a formal recommendation, leaving the decision to the board. The next meeting was tentatively scheduled for May 20th. A resident asked whether the business was on Phoenix Drive but the applicant confirmed it was on Schneider Road. The board discussed but did not formally adopt specific time limits for guest stays.
title: BZA 4-15-24
